title: Alexander Ramlie’s estate planning revealed, AMMN’s price soars
body: The stock price of PT Amman Mineral Internasional Tbk (AMMN) went up 5.29% until the end of today’s (26/6) afternoon session.

title: Profit declined, MUTU stays true to its promise of 30% dividend allocation
body: PT Mutuagung Lestari Tbk (MUTU) fulfils its promise stated in its IPO prospectus last year by allocating dividend of 30.45% of its 2023 net profit despite 15.8% year-on-year (yoy) contraction.

title: PMMP to hold rights issue of 905.90 million shares
body: PT Panca Mitra Multiperdana Tbk (PMMP) will hold a rights issue of a maximum 905.90 million shares of IDR 100 each.

title: Up 20%, MDKI to distribute dividend of IDR 30.36 billion
body: PT Emdeki Utama Tbk (MDKI) increases its dividend by 20% to IDR 30.36 billion for the fiscal year (FY) 2023 from IDR 25.30 billion for FY 2022.

title: Panin Sekuritas to share cash dividend of IDR 128.05 billion
body: PT Panin Sekuritas Tbk (PANS), a security company of PT Bank Pan Indonesia Tbk (PNBN), plans a cash dividend distribution worth IDR 128.05 billion or IDR 180 per share.

title: SAPX secures revolving loan of IDR 125 billion
body: PT Satria Antaran Prima Tbk (SAPX) managed to obtain a revolving loan facility of IDR 125 billion from PT Bank Permata Tbk (BNLI) yesterday (25/6).

title: Waskita Karya to inject IDR 10 billion for Kapal Betung Toll Road
body: PT Waskita Karya (Persero) Tbk (WSKT), through its subsidiary, PT Waskita Toll Road (WTR), has channelled extra capital worth IDR 10 billion to PT Waskita Sriwijaya Toll (WST), a business entity that owns the concession of Kayu Agung–Palembang–Betung (Kapal Betung) Toll Road.

title: Trimegah Bangun Persada to raise buyback budget to IDR 1 trillion
body: PT Trimegah Bangun Persada Tbk (NCKL) has increased the allocated fund for share buyback to IDR 1 trillion from the initial IDR 400 billion.

title: Continuing to release Totalindo (TOPS) shares, Deputy Attorney General for Special Crimes now only holds 5.79 percent
body: The Deputy Attorney General for Special Crimes of the Prosecutor's Office of the Republic of Indonesia had not stopped selling shares of PT Totalindo Eka Persada Tbk (TOPS), an issuer of building construction. On Friday, June 21, this investor released an additional 70 million shares, bringing its total number of shares held to 1.93 billion, or approximately 5.79%. Besides that, Tan John Tanuwijaya, the majority shareholder in PT Berdikari Pondasi Perkasa Tbk (BDKR), was still selling shares, this time for 27.54 million. His share percentage was recorded as being around 58.89%. PT Precise Pacific Realty was also recorded as selling 20 million shares of the tourism services company PT Sona Topas Tourism Industry Tbk (SONA), with the remaining shares amounting to approximately 31.65%.

title: Distribute financing to 900 thousand MSMEs, KoinWorks launches Impact Report 2023
body: KoinWorks, a peer-to-peer (P2P) lending fintech that was founded in 2016, has just launched the 2023 Impact Report which displays the company's social impact throughout 2023 after distributing financing facilities to more than 900 thousand MSMEs (Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises).

title: Alexander Ramlie’s estate planning revealed, AMMN’s price soars
body: The stock price of PT Amman Mineral Internasional Tbk (AMMN) went up 5.29% until the end of today’s (26/6) afternoon session.

body: PT Mutuagung Lestari Tbk (MUTU) fulfils its promise stated in its IPO prospectus last year by allocating dividend of 30.45% of its 2023 net profit despite 15.8% year-on-year (yoy) contraction.

body: PT Panca Mitra Multiperdana Tbk (PMMP) will hold a rights issue of a maximum 905.90 million shares of IDR 100 each.

body: PT Emdeki Utama Tbk (MDKI) increases its dividend by 20% to IDR 30.36 billion for the fiscal year (FY) 2023 from IDR 25.30 billion for FY 2022.

body: PT Panin Sekuritas Tbk (PANS), a security company of PT Bank Pan Indonesia Tbk (PNBN), plans a cash dividend distribution worth IDR 128.05 billion or IDR 180 per share.

body: PT Satria Antaran Prima Tbk (SAPX) managed to obtain a revolving loan facility of IDR 125 billion from PT Bank Permata Tbk (BNLI) yesterday (25/6).

body: PT Waskita Karya (Persero) Tbk (WSKT), through its subsidiary, PT Waskita Toll Road (WTR), has channelled extra capital worth IDR 10 billion to PT Waskita Sriwijaya Toll (WST), a business entity that owns the concession of Kayu Agung–Palembang–Betung (Kapal Betung) Toll Road.

title: Trimegah Bangun Persada to raise buyback budget to IDR 1 trillion
body: PT Trimegah Bangun Persada Tbk (NCKL) has increased the allocated fund for share buyback to IDR 1 trillion from the initial IDR 400 billion.

title: Continuing to release Totalindo (TOPS) shares, Deputy Attorney General for Special Crimes now only holds 5.79 percent
body: The Deputy Attorney General for Special Crimes of the Prosecutor's Office of the Republic of Indonesia had not stopped selling shares of PT Totalindo Eka Persada Tbk (TOPS), an issuer of building construction. On Friday, June 21, this investor released an additional 70 million shares, bringing its total number of shares held to 1.93 billion, or approximately 5.79%. Besides that, Tan John Tanuwijaya, the majority shareholder in PT Berdikari Pondasi Perkasa Tbk (BDKR), was still selling shares, this time for 27.54 million. His share percentage was recorded as being around 58.89%. PT Precise Pacific Realty was also recorded as selling 20 million shares of the tourism services company PT Sona Topas Tourism Industry Tbk (SONA), with the remaining shares amounting to approximately 31.65%.

title: Distribute financing to 900 thousand MSMEs, KoinWorks launches Impact Report 2023
body: KoinWorks, a peer-to-peer (P2P) lending fintech that was founded in 2016, has just launched the 2023 Impact Report which displays the company's social impact throughout 2023 after distributing financing facilities to more than 900 thousand MSMEs (Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises).

title: PBRX in PKPU status
body: Commercial Court of Central Jakarta approved the plea of Suspension of Debt Payment Obligation (PKPU) over PT Pan Brothers Tbk (PBRX) submitted by PT Januardi Putera Logistik (JPL).

title: Kedoya Adyaraya to set up final dividend of IDR 10.22 billion
body: PT Kedoya Adyaraya Tbk (RSGK), the operator of Grha Kedoya Hospital and Grha MM2100 Hospital, will distribute final dividend of IDR 10.22 billion or IDR 11 per share.

title: Tan John Tanuwijaya now only owns 62.45% of Berdikari Foundation (BDKR)
body: Tan John Tanuwijaya, the controlling shareholder in the foundation specialty company PT Berdikari Pondasi Perkasa Tbk (BDKR), released an additional 34.36 million shares on Tuesday, June 11. This investor's shareholding continues to fall as a result of his regular share sales; only 62.45% remains.

title: Net foreign volume stuck in red zone with -1.22 billion shares
body: The net foreign volume remained in the red zone, on Wednesday, June 12, totaling -1.22 billion shares. This figure was obtained from the difference in foreign buying volume, which was only 3 billion shares, and foreign selling volume, which reached 4.23 billion shares. The most shares sold belonged to the digital company PT GoTo Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO), namely 2.29 billion shares, with a net volume of -703.12 million shares.

title: Prajogo Pangestu acquires two billion Barito Pacific (BRPT) shares
body: Prajogo Pangestu, the controlling shareholder of PT Barito Pacific Tbk (BRPT), added another 2 billion shares of the company, which operates in the energy and industrial sectors, to his holdings on Monday, June 10. Prajogo previously held approximately 69.05% of the shares but now owns 71.19%. Prajogo purchased shares through two securities companies: BUT Standard Chartered Bank and PT Bahana Sekuritas.

title: Foreign selling volume surpasses 4 billion shares, net foreign volume drops to -1.13 billion shares
body: Share sales by foreign investors on Tuesday, June 11, resulted in foreign selling volume reaching 4.06 billion shares. In addition, foreign investors also carried out share purchase transactions and recorded a foreign purchase volume of 2.93 billion shares. This huge disparity caused net foreign volume to drop to -1.13 billion shares. The digital company PT GoTo Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO) is still ranked #1 on the top sell list, having sold 2.22 billion shares for a net volume of -762.99 million.

title: Reliancever Holdings invest in two MNC Group issuers
body: After becoming a new PT MNC Kapital (BCAP) investor, Reliancever Holdings Inc. entered two other MNC Group issuers on Friday, June 7. First, this foreign investor bought 9.16 billion shares of the issuer, which operates in the office space rental sector, PT MNC Land Tbk (KPIG), which accounted for around 9.39% of the shares. Furthermore, approximately 5.79 billion shares of PT MNC Asia Holding Tbk (BHIT), a holding company in the investment sector, were purchased, resulting in 6.74% ownership. Even so, Reliance decided to sell approximately 96.19 million shares of financial services company BACP this time. All transactions were conducted through PT MNC Sekuritas.

title: Investor: OJK and IDX failed to exercise optimum supervision towards TIFA
body: Retail investors highlighted OJK and the stock exchange authority’s lack of effort in protecting investors towards PT KDB Tifa Finance Tbk (TIFA) with its free float rate of only 0.35% and the lack of dividend distribution in the past 5 years.

title: SCCO saw 103% surge in dividend
body: The dividend of PT Supreme Cable Manufacturing & Commerce Tbk (SCCO) soared 103% to IDR 61.67 billion for the fiscal year (FY) 2023 although the profit slightly declined from 2022.

title: Cipta Perdana Lancar to hold IPO, offer 25% shares
body: PT Cipta Perdana Lancar Tbk (PART), a spare part and automotive component manufacturer, will issue 680 million of its shares in Indonesia Stock Exchange (IDX) through an initial public offering (IPO).

title: GOTO reported the furthest drop among BUKA and BELI
body: PT GoTo Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO), the managing company of Gojek platform, reported the most significant drop amongst two of its competitors in e-commerce application industry.

title: Expanding all business lines, Moratelindo to set up capex of IDR 1 trillion
body: PT Mora Telematika Indonesia Tbk (MORA), more popularly known as Moratelindo, is budgeting capital expenditure (capex) worth IDR 1 trillion for 2024.

title: GOTO denies terminating 80% of Tokopedia’s services
body: PT GoTo Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO) denies the information regarding the company’s plan to stop up to 80% of Tokopedia’s service, its e-commerce platform, in the near future.

title: RMKE net profit declines 64.48% in first quarter
body: PT RMK Energy Tbk (RMKE), an issuer providing coal transportation services, only posted a net profit of IDR 44.58 billion in the first quarter (Q1) 2024. This profit decreased by 64.48% year-on-year (yoy) or from the same quarter last year.

title: Salim Group to acquire land of 18,290 Ha in Padalarang
body: PT Inti Cakrawala Citra (ICC), a Salim Group company, is acquiring a land of 18,290 hectares (Ha) for IDR 80 billion in Padalarang, West Java, from PT Falmaco Nonwoven Industri Tbk (FLMC).

title: Three textile companies suffered capital deficiency
body: Several issuers in the garment and textile industries have reportedly been at capital deficiency throughout 2023.

title: In need of extra capital, Bank Victoria International to issue bond of IDR 500 billion
body: PT Bank Victoria International Tbk (BVIC) will issue its Shelf-Registration Subordinated Bond III of BVIC Phase I Year 2024 with total principal of IDR 500 million.

title: Unilever Indonesia divests assets for IDR 57.6 billion
body: PT Unilever Indonesia Tbk (UNVR) has divested its asset, which is an ice cream production machine, for a transaction value worth EUR 3.27 million or equal to approximately IDR 57.6 billion.

title: Indonesia Kendaraan Terminal to channel out dividend of IDR 113.45 billion, up 25%
body: PT Indonesia Kendaraan Terminal Tbk (IPCC), an operator of Tanjung Priok port, will distribute cash dividend of IDR 113.45 billion taken out of its 2023 net profit.

title: Last SUN auction’s incoming bids hit IDR 42.96 trillion
body: The total incoming bids in the auction of Government Securities (lit. Surat Utang Negara/SUN) last Tuesday, June 11, 2024, reached IDR 42.96 trillion.

title: Utama Radar Cahaya to distribute stock dividend of IDR 5.43 billion
body: PT Utama Radar Cahaya Tbk (RCCC), a logistics and shipping company debuted on Indonesia Stock Exchange (IDX) circa 2022, will distribute cash dividend worth IDR 5.43 billion.

title: TRIM to issue Bonds of IDR 388 billion
body: PT Trimegah Sekuritas Indonesia Tbk (TRIM) will issue Shelf-Registration Bond I Phase II Year 2024 worth IDR 388 billion in the next couple of weeks, from June 25 to 26, 2024.

title: Preparing for potential demand increase, SUNI to set up factory operating in Q1 2025
body: PT Sunindo Pratama Tbk (SUNI) is focusing the allocation of capital expenditure (capex) of IDR 327 billion for the construction of the second factory of PT Rainbow Tubulars Manufacture (RTM), its subsidiary, in Batam, in 2024.

title: The net foreign volume plunges to -3.14 billion shares as many GOTO shares released
body: On Monday, June 10, foreign investors sold a considerable number of shares in the digital company PT GoTo Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO). A total of 5.18 billion GOTO shares were sold, for a net volume of -2.60 billion shares. These share sales therefore significantly boosted foreign sales volume, which rose to 6.99 billion shares. Meanwhile, foreign buying volume was only 3.85 billion shares, causing the net foreign volume to drop to -3.14 billion shares.

title: Singapore Govt acquires 1.15 billion shares of Bank Jago (ARTO)
body: On Thursday, June 6, the Government of Singapore acquired 1.15 billion shares of digital banking company PT Bank Jago Tbk (ARTO) via Citibank N.A. and BUT Standard Chartered Bank. This new investor gained approximately 8.34% of ARTO shares as a result of this purchase. A new investor, Yasir Arafat Rumagiar, has also recently entered the chicken farming and slaughterhouse issuer PT Janu Putra Sejahtera Tbk (AYAM), purchasing 201.63 million shares. This individual investor immediately owns 5.04% of the shares.

title: Stock dropped 620% since IPO, GOTO sets up IDR 32 trillion for buyback
body: Shareholders of PT GoTo Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO) agreed upon the allocation of IDR 32 trillion worth of budget to repurchase the issued shares (buyback).

title: SUNI to distribute dividend of IDR 11 billion this July
body: PT Sunindo Pratama Tbk (SUNI), an OCTG pipe manufacturer, is preparing to distribute dividend of IDR 11 billion for the fiscal year (FY) 2023 on July 12, 2024.

title: ZYRX’s dividend down 58.19% due to profit slippage
body: The dividend of PT Zyrexindo Mandiri Buana Tbk (ZYRX) for the fiscal year (FY) 2023 shrank 58.19% compared to the same period in 2022.

title: Securing portion in VLS, UANG channels IDR 220.65 billion
body: PT Pakuan Tbk (CUAN) channels capital worth IDR 220.65 billion for 441,303 shares of IDR 500 thousand each in PT Vasamty Land Sawangan (VLS), its joint venture established alongside Samty Asia Investments Pte Ltd (SAI).

title: Adding new business line, CMRY to invest IDR 292.58 billion
body: PT Cisarua Mountain Dairy Tbk (CMRY) sets up investment of IDR 292.58 billion to add 14 new business lines.

title: Nearly a year debuting, AMMN saw 582.30% surge in stock price
body: As of yesterday (11/6), the stock of PT Amman Mineral Internasional Tbk (AMMN) soared 582.03% since its debut in the stock market on July 7, 2023.

title: Acquiring Probiotec, PYFA’s subsidiary receives IDR 1.07 trillion
body: PYFA Australia Pty Ltd (PAPL), an entity under PT Pyridam Farma Tbk (PYFA), has secured extra capital of USD 99.57 million or equal to approximately IDR 1.07 trillion.

title: DEPO to share dividend of IDR 27.16 billion, 73.9% higher now
body: PT Caturkarda Depo Bangunan Tbk (DEPO), a retail company specialized in building materials, is planning a cash dividend distribution of IDR 27.16 billion for the fiscal year (FY) 2023.

title: Concluding IPO, Benteng Api Technic to acquireland for IDR 24.9 billion
body: PT Benteng Api Technic Tbk (BATR), a fire resistant material and heat insulation manufacturer, has conducted several assets in the form of land and building to support operational activities.

title: Ace Hardware to share cash dividend of IDR 573.53 billion
body: PT Ace Hardware Tbk (ACES), a retail company under Kawan Lama Group, will distribute cash dividend of IDR 573.53 billion or IDR 33.5 per share taken out of its 2023 net profit.

title: SSIA divested 2% of SCS for IDR 169.81 billion
body: PT Surya Semesta Internusa Tbk (SSIA) is divesting 2% or 55.80 million of its shares in PT Suryacipta Swadaya (SCS) for IDR 169.81 billion to PT Puri Bumi Lestari (PBL).

title: Eyeing 7% growth, DVLA prepares capex of IDR 209 billion
body: PT Darya-Varia Laboratoria Tbk (DVLA) is reportedly preparing capital expenditure (capex) budget worth IDR 209 billion for 2024, which will mostly be allocated for expansion.

title: Distributing another IDR 65, Darya-Varia’s dividend clocks up to IDR 108 per share
body: PT Darya-Varia Laboratoria Tbk (DVLA) sets up IDR 65 per share for the final dividend of the fiscal year (FY) 2023, totalling IDR 72.8 billion, which will be taken out of its current year’s net profit in 2023.

title: NISP and Bank Commonwealth to merge this year
body: PT Bank OCBC NISP Tbk (NISP) and PT Bank Commonwealth (BC) will merge after completing 100% of the acquisition of BC’s shares.

title: Sariguna Primatirta to establish another subsidiary, focusing on ice
body: PT Sariguna Primatirta Tbk (CLEO) has established a new subsidiary called PT Stube Segarkan Indonesia (SSI), with a business line with the focus on ice production.

title: Securing fresh funds, PTRO now starts its climb
body: PT Petrosea Tbk (PTRO) was seen climbing 11% in the morning session after securing capital funds from PT Kreasi Jasa Persada (KJP), a subsidiary of PT Petrindo Jasa Kreasi Tbk (CUAN), last week (7/6).

title: Reliancever Holdings invests 2.49 billion shares in MNC Kapital (BCAP)
body: Reliancever Holdings Inc., a foreign company based in the British Virgin Islands, purchased up to 2.49 billion shares of PT MNC Kapital Indonesia Tbk (BCAP) on Wednesday, June 5. Following this transaction with PT MNC Sekuritas, Reliancever became the newest holder of the financial services company's shares by 5.85%.

title: Debuting, Tanoko’s Hebel manufacturer to issue 15.05% of shares in IPO
body: PT Superior Prima Sukses Tbk (BLES), a Hebel and mortar manufacturer based in Surabaya, plans to issue 1.31 billion shares in Initial Public Offering (IPO).

title: INCO to allocate capex of IDR 1.46 trillion from rights issue proceeds
body: PT Vale Indonesia Tbk (INCO) is allocating 79.34% or IDR 1.46 trillion of total rights issue proceeds, which involves the issuance of 603.4 million shares on the next two weeks (24/6).

title: Bank Syariah Indonesia to offer sukuk mudharabah of IDR 3 trillion
body: PT Bank Syariah Indonesia Tbk (BRIS), a state-owned sharia bank, is aiming to score fresh funds of IDR 3 trillion through the issuance of Shelf-Registration Sukuk Mudharabah I Phase I Year 2024.

title: Puradelta Lestari distributes cash dividend of IDR 578 billion, up 19.9%
body: PT Puradelta Lestari Tbk (DMAS), a real estate management and developer, is issuing cash dividend of IDR 578 billion or IDR 12 per share for the fiscal year (FY) 2023.

title: KEEN to channel dividend of IDR 27 billion
body: PT Kencana Energi Lestari Tbk (KEEN) will distribute dividend for the fiscal year (FY) 2023 of IDR 27.67 billion, higher than IDR 23.83 billion seen in FY 2022.

title: Continuing land acquisition, PWON spent IDR 121.7 billion
body: PT Pakuwon Permai (PP), a subsidiary of PT Pakuwon Jati Tbk (PWON), is acquiring a land of 52,040 hectares (Ha) in Semarang, Central Java, worth IDR 121.7 billion.

title: Optimistic, VAST targets 30% revenue growth
body: After concluding the acquisition of a warehouse in Tangerang last year, PT Vastland Indonesia Tbk (VAST), a developer focusing in logistics and warehousing, is targeting its revenue to grow 30% year-on-year (yoy) in 2024.

title: Atlantis Subsea Indonesia obtains 2 new contracts from Timas Suplindo
body: PT Atlantis Subsea Indonesia Tbk (ATLA), a maritime survey and inspection company debuted on Indonesia Stock Exchange (IDX) last April, has already secured 2 new contracts from Timas Suplindo.

title: Divesting portion in PYFA, RGI rakes in IDR 5.09 billion
body: Rejuve Global Investment (RGI) Pte Ltd secures IDR 56.09 billion from divesting 3.56% of PT Pyridam Farma Tbk’s (PYFA) shares alst week (6/6).

title: JKON to set up dividend of IDR 69.31 billion, an increase from last year
body: PT Jaya Konstruksi Manggala Pratama Tbk (JKON) is allocating 29.18% or IDR 69.31 billion of total net profit in 2023, of IDR 237.47 billion, as dividend.

title: Soraya Berjaya Indonesia to offer 240 million shares in IPO
body: PT Soraya Berjaya Indonesia Tbk (SPRE), a bed cover and household equipment manufacturer, will issue 240 million of its shares through initial public offering (IPO).

title: Profit down 35.6%, Mega Perintis’s dividend shrank
body: PT Mega Perintis Tbk (ZONE), a company engaged in the fashion production and retail, only distributed cash dividend of IDR 14.05 billion or IDR 16.15 per share taken out of its net profit in 2023.

title: Strengthening capital structure, BPD Jabar to issue Bond of IDR 3 trillion
body: PT Bank Pembangunan Daerah Jawa Barat dan Banten Tbk (BJBR), starting this June, will issue its Subordinated Bond Year 2024 worth IDR 1.5 trillion.

title: PTPP obtains IDR 8.9 trillion new contracts until May 2024
body: PT PP (Persero) Tbk (PTPP), a state-owned construction and investment company, has secured new contracts worth IDR 8.9 trillion since the beginning of this year until May 2024 (5M).

title: Opening bell saw JCI sank to the red zone
body: Jakarta Composite Index slowed down towards the red zone on Monday (10/6) morning.

title: Foreign buy volume failed to reach 2 billion, net foreign volume closed at 449.64 million shares
body: Foreign share purchases on Friday, June 7, fell short of 2 billion shares, hovering around 1.94 billion shares. Meanwhile, foreign selling volume was 2.39 billion shares, resulting in a net foreign volume of -449.64 million shares. Following the sale of 265.03 million shares, the banking business PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia (Persero) Tbk (BBRI) had the greatest net volume in the top sell list at -204.73 million shares.

title: Gojek Tokopedia share sales contributed 50% of foreign sales volume
body: On Thursday, June 6, sales of digital firm share at PT GoTo Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO) accounted for 50% of total foreign sales volume (3.07 billion shares), with foreign investors selling 1.51 billion shares for a net volume of -849.93 million shares. Foreign buying volume was 2.14 billion shares, resulting in a net foreign volume of -935.91 million shares.

title: CHIP’s dividend grew 32.9%; market cap soared 1,002%
body: The dividend of PT Pelita Teknologi Global Tbk (CHIP) increased 32.09% in the fiscal year (FY) 2023 from the FY 2022.

title: Indonesia Fibreboard Industry readies cash dividend of IDR 47.06 billion
body: PT Indonesia Fibreboard Industry Tbk (IFII) will distribute cash dividend of IDR 47.06 billion or IDR 5 per share taken out of its net profit of 2023.

title: AMMN’s market cap surpasses BREN
body: Market capitalisation of PT Amman Mineral Internasional Tbk (AMMN) arrives at the runner-up spot in Top 10 Market Cap in the stock exchange following the decline of PT Barito Renewables Energy Tbk (BREN) due to full call auction (FCA) system.

title: SUN auction is back next week, eyeing IDR 22 trillion this time
body: The Indonesian government will once again conduct an auction of Government Securities (SUN) next Tuesday (11/6) with an indicated target of IDR 22 trillion.

title: No dividend planned; these are HRUM’s plan in nickel sector
body: Annual General Meeting of Shareholders (AGMS) of PT Harum Energy Tbk (HRUM) today (7/6) agreed not to distribute dividend for the fiscal year (FY) 2023, ending its 3-year streak of dividend distribution since 2021.

title: BREN stumbled again; market cap dropped by IDR 86.96 trillion
body: Stock price of PT Barito Renewables Energy Tbk (BREN) plummeted 9.70% from IDR 6,700 to IDR 6,050 until the closing day of today’s (7/6) morning session.

title: Indomobil Finance will issue bonds worth IDR 2.83 trillion
body: PT Indomobil Finance Indonesia, a financing venture under PT Indomobil Multi Jasa Tbk (IMJS), will issue Shelf-Registration Bond V Phase III Year 2024 with total principal of IDR 2.83 trillion.

title: Jaya Real Property readies dividend of IDR 330 billion
body: PT Jaya Real Property Tbk (JRPT), a real estate developer and management, will distribute cash dividend of IDR 330 billion or IDR 24 per share.

title: Shareholders approve PUDP’s stock split plan with 1:2 ratio
body: PT Pudjiadi Prestige Tbk (PUDP) prepares a stock split with ratio set to 1:2 at the beginning of July 2024 after securing approval from shareholders during its Extraordinary General Meeting of Shareholders last Thursday (6/6).

title: SPTO to share dividend of IDR 135 billion
body: PT Surya Pertiwi Tbk (SPTO) distributes dividend for the fiscal year (FY) 2023 worth IDR 135 billion, relatively stable compared to the dividend for FY 2022.

title: Out of monitoring board, INPP works on bond issuance in second half of 2024
body: As of May 27, 2024, PT Indonesian Paradise Property Tbk (INPP) is no longer listed under Special Monitoring Board of Indonesia Stock Exchange (IDX), and is now entering the Development Board.

title: Transcoal Pacific put old boats on sale, now divesting 1 unit for IDR 8 billion
body: PT Transcoal Pacific Tbk (TCPI) has reportedly divested 1 unit of motor vessels called Varuna 01 for a transaction value worth IDR 8 billion.

title: ULTJ listed as top gainers
body: The stock of PT Ultra Jaya Milk Industry & Trading Company Tbk (ULTJ) rose 6.67%, arriving as one of Top 10 Gainers in yesterday’s (6/6) session.

title: Alam Sutera retains earnings of IDR 639.1 billion
body: Annual General Meeting of Shareholders (AGMS) of PT Alam Sutera Realty Tbk (ASRI) agreed to hold off net profit of 2023 of IDR 639.1 billion as retained earnings.

title: JCI slowed down throughout the day
body: Jakarta Composite Index has yet to rebound during today’s (6/6) session to its position seen earlier this week (3-4/6) at above 7,000. Today, the shift is relatively slow compared to yesterday.

title: Pudjiadi Prestige to ready dividend of IDR 10 per share late June
body: Annual General Meeting of Shareholders of PT Pudjiadi Prestige Tbk (PUDP) approved the plan of dividend distribution of IDR 10 per share for the fiscal year (FY) 2023.

title: Indonesian Paradise Property to start Balikpapan project next year
body: Joining the hype in IKN, PT Indonesian Paradise Property Tbk (INPP) is getting ready to start its latest project in Balikpapan, which is revealed to be a hotel.

title: MNC Kapital issues bond worth IDR 390 billion
body: PT MNC Kapital Indonesia Tbk (BCAP) will issue Shelf-Registration Bond IV Phase II Year 2024 with total principal of IDR 390 billion.

title: Ace Hardware to spend IDR 30 billion for digital platform
body: PT Ace Hardware Tbk (ACES), a special retailer under Kawan Lama Group, has channelled extra capital worth IDR 30 billion to one of its subsidiaries, PT Omni Digitama Internusa (ODI).

title: Megapratama Karya annexes 10% of Siloam Hospitals' (SILO) shares
body: On Tuesday, June 4, market hints began with PT Megapratama Karya Persada purchasing 1.35 billion shares of health service provider PT Siloam International Hospitals Tbk (SILO). Following the completion of the transaction facilitated by PT Ciptadana Sekuritas Asia, this new investor received an immediate share ownership of 10.4%. On the other hand, the controller of PT Hotel Candi Baru made a large-scale share reduction by selling 7.84 billion shares of the herbal medicine and pharmaceutical company PT Industri Jamu Dan Farmasi Sido Muncul Tbk (SIDO). This investor previously owned 68.14% but now only owns approximately 42%.

title: Net foreign volume remains in red zone at -513.29 million shares
body: Foreign selling volume on Wednesday, June 5, was still larger at 3.03 billion shares than foreign buying volume, which was 2.51 billion. As a result, net foreign volume remained unchanged in the red zone, closing at -513.29 million shares. The telecoms business PT Telkom Indonesia (Persero) Tbk (TLKM) had the most shares purchased, with 110.02 million shares and a net volume of 30.24 million shares. Meanwhile, the digital company PT GoTo Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO) continued to hold the most shares sold, with 967.83 million shares and a net volume of -123.56 million shares.

title: Full call auction in effect, BREN’s market cap cut by IDR 608.72 trillion
body: The stock price of PT Barito Renewables Energy Tbk (BREN) continues to decline under watchlist board following full call auction (FCA) policy that was put in place last week (29/5).

title: AMMN consistently reach top three in market cap
body: PT Amman Mineral Internasional Tbk (AMMN)'s market capitalization (market cap) climbed to the top three in Top Market Capitalization as of May 2024.

title: IMG Sejahtera Langgeng secures syndicated loan of USD 450 million
body: PT IMG Sejahtera Langgeng, a subsidiary of Indomobil Group, has received syndicated loan worth USD 450 million in order to support electric vehicle (EV) development.

title: Digital Mediatama Maxima to offer 432.87 million treasury shares
body: PT Digital Mediatama Maxima Tbk (DMMX), an information technology (IT) service and consultancy, will sell off 432.87 million shares or 5.63% of the shares gained from a buyback.

title: Increasing liquidity, Merdeka Battery Materials to conduct rights issue of 10%
body: PT Merdeka Battery Materials Tbk (MBMA), a nickel mining company under PT Merdeka Copper Gold Tbk (MDKA), plans to issue 10% of new shares under rights issue scheme to increase liquidity.

title: Net foreign volume drops to -1.18 billion shares
body: Foreign share sales appear to have increased on Tuesday, June 4 but were not followed by share purchases. The gap between the foreign buying volume of 3.26 billion shares and foreign selling volume of 4.45 billion shares resulted in net foreign volume falling to -1.18 billion shares. PT GoTo Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO) shares remained in first place in the top sell list, unchanged from the previous day, with 2.25 billion shares sold and a net volume of -1.24 billion shares.

title: Profit declined, BMHS saw lowest dividend in the past three years
body: PT Bundamedik Tbk (BMHS), the managing company of Rumah Sakit Bunda Hospital network, only distributes cash dividend of IDR 0.56 per share or IDR 4.82 billion for the fiscal year (FY) 2023.

title: JCI spends the day at red zone
body: Jakarta Composite Index must spend the entire day in the red zone until the closing bell today (5/6), ending its robust strides for two consecutive days in the green zone since earlier this Monday (3/6).

title: PPRO suffered loss, no dividend in sight
body: Annual General Meeting of Shareholders of PT PP Properti Tbk (PPRO) agreed not to distribute dividend to shareholders following a loss recorded in 2023 of IDR 1.28 trillion.

title: MNC Media released 335 million MNC Digital (MSIN) shares
body: On Monday, June 3, media holding company PT Media Nusantara Citra Tbk (MNC Media) released approximately 335.81 million shares of one of its digital media subsidiaries, PT MNC Digital Entertainment Tbk (MSIN). This investor released up to 2.5% of his share ownership through PT MNC Sekuritas, leaving it with only 5.5%.

title: Sinar Nusantara acquires 5.94% stake in Resources Alam (KKGI)
body: At the end of May, the coal mining company PT Resource Alam Indonesia Tbk (KKGI) welcomed a new foreign investor, Sinar Nusantara Sdn Bhd. On Friday, May 31, approximately 296.96 million shares of this coal mining company were purchased, making Sinar Nusantara the new owner of 5.94% of the shares. In addition, Dimas Adiarso Putro purchased 53.78 million shares of PT Anugerah Spareparts Sejahtera Tbk (AEGS), an issuer in the motor vehicle filter industry, to acquire a new 5.35% stake.

title: Bids recorded in last state sukuk auction reaches IDR 26.21 trillion
body: The total incoming bids for Government Sharia Securities (SBSN) or state sukuk last Tuesday (4/6) reportedly reached IDR 26.21 trillion.

title: Foreign investors sell Gojek Tokopedia shares, net foreign volume closed at -965.20 million shares
body: Foreign investors initiated share sales transactions on Monday, June 3, by selling 1.49 billion shares of the digital company PT GoTo Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO), which resulted in a net volume of -799.07 million shares. This sum contributed to the foreign sales volume, which was around 3.72 billion shares. Meanwhile, foreign buying volume was 2.75 billion shares, with net foreign volume closing at -965.20 million shares.

title: Reksa Dana Syariah Pacific acquired 5.79% of Bumi Citra Permai (BCIP)
body: Reksa Dana Syariah Pacific Saham Syariah III purchased approximately 82.92 million shares of real estate company PT Bumi Citra Permai Tbk (BCIP) on Thursday, May 30. The purchase through PT Bank Danamon Indonesia Tbk then resulted in the new investor acquiring approximately 5.79% ownership of BCIP.

title: Millennium Pharmacon sets up its highest dividend in the apdt 5 years
body: PT Millennium Pharmacon Tbk (SDPC), a retailer of pharmaceutical product and medical devices, will distribute cash dividend of IDR 2.5 per share or IDR 3.18 billion in total.

title: Profit down, DSNG insisted on dividend of IDR 22 per share
body: Shareholders of PT Dharma Satya Nusantara Tbk (DSNG) agreed upon the plan of distributing cash dividend for the fiscal year (FY) 2023 of IDR 22 per share, although its net profit sank 30.42% year-on-year (yoy).

title: Bank Amar to distribute first dividend in June 2024
body: PT Bank Amar Indonesia Tbk (AMAR) has become the first digital bank to distribute cash dividend to its shareholders for the fiscal year 2023, amounting to IDR 55 billion or approximately IDR 3.03 per share.

title: BPII’s dividend shifted 14.42% down due to profit shrinkage
body: PT Batavia Prosperindo Internasional Tbk (BPII) is allocating a total dividend of IDR 78.08 billion for the fiscal year (FY) 2023, down 14.42% from FY 2022 dividend of IDR 91.24 billion.

title: RMK Energy’s net profit plummeted 64.48% in Q1 2024
body: PT RMK Energy Tbk (RMKE), a coal mining company owned by RMK Investama, recorded net profit of IDR 44.58 billion in Q1 2024.

title: JCI weakens at Tuesday morning
body: Jakarta Composite Index (JCI) entered the red zone as of 10.51 AM WIB. After the opening bell today (5/6), the index shifted through the red zone, weakening from its closing position yesterday (4/6).

title: AKRA grants subsidiary IDR 81.4 billion capital
body: PT AKR Corporindo Tbk (AKRA), a fuel distributor and managing company of industrial zone, has channelled extra capital to one of its subsidiaries, Chemical Petroleoum International Trading Pte. Ltd. (CPIT).

title: Sejahtera Raya transfers 25 million Indomobil (IMAS) shares to Trimegah Sekuritas
body: On Wednesday, May 29, up to 25 million shares of the automotive company PT Indomobil Sukses Internasional Tbk (IMAS) traded hands. PT Sejahtera Raya Perkasa, which owns 10.1% of IMAS, transferred the shares to PT Trimegah Sekuritas Indonesia Tbk, which now also owns approximately 10.14% of the shares. Aside from that, PT Metro Pacific Tollways Indonesia acquired nearly 80 million shares of PT Nusantara Infrastructure Tbk (META), a construction contractor, increasing the controlling share percentage from 95.92% to 95.97%.

title: Iforte to acquire 90.11% of IBST
body: PT Iforte Solusi Infotek (iForte) in under process of acquiring 90.11% of PT Inti Bangun Sejahtera Tbk (IBST), primarily owned by PT Bakti Taruna Sejati (BTS).

title: Elnusa to channel IDR 82 billion to pipe manufacturing business line
body: PT Elnusa Tbk (ELSA), an issuer engaged in the support of upstream oil and gas business, has channelled a loan facility worth IDR 82 billion to one of its subsidiaries, PT Elnusa Fabrikasi Konstruksi (EFK).

title: GGRP officially divested 76.07% of NBP for USD 338.8 million
body: PT Gunung Raja Paksi Tbk (GGRP), one of the biggest steel manufacturers in Indonesia, has reportedly divested 76.07% of PT Nusantara Baja Profil (NBP).

title: JCI gains strength until the closing bell
body: Jakarta Composite Index (JCI) arrived at 7,009.31 at today’s (4/6) closing bell, higher than last closing position at 7,036.19 yesterday (3/6). JCI’s upturn has been detected since the opening bell this morning.

title: Mulia Industrindo to distribute dividend of IDR 99.22 billion
body: PT Mulia Industrindo Tbk (MLIA), a manufacturer and distributor of glass and ceramic tiles, decides on a cash dividend distribution of IDR 99.22 billion or IDR 15 per share that will be taken out of its 2023 net profit.

title: Profit keeps proliferating, Allo Bank still hesitant on dividend distribution
body: PT Allo Bank Indonesia Tbk (BBHI) decided not to distribute any dividend despite its growth following shares’ acquisition in mid-2021. The entire of its net profit of 2023 is allocated to enhance its capitalisation.

title: Merdeka Battery Materials booked net profit of USD 3.67 million in Q1
body: PT Merdeka Battery Materials Tbk (MBMA), a nickel mining company under Merdeka Copper Gold, reported net profit of USD 3.67 million in the first quarter (Q1) of 2024.

title: KRAS saw a downturn in 2023
body: PT Krakatau Steel (Persero) Tbk (KRAS) showed declining performance throughout 2023 compared to 2022.

title: Tower Bersama Infrastructure to distribute cash dividend of IDR 683.62 billion
body: PT Tower Bersama Infrastructure Tbk (TBIG), a telecommunication tower provider, will allocate cash dividend of IDR 683.62 billion from its net profit in the fiscal year (FY) 2023.

title: Adi Sarana Armada to channel IDR 2.2 billion capital to logistics line
body: PT Adi Sarana Armada Tbk (ASSA), a transportation and logistics company, has reportedly allocated capital injection worth IDR 2.2 billion to one of its subsidiaries engaged in the logistics service, PT Adi Sarana Logistik (ASL).

title: Up 1,201% since acquisition, PANI to set up private placement
body: The stock of PT Pantai Indah Kapuk 2 Tbk (PANI) has skyrocketed by up to 1,201% since taken over by Agung Sedayu and Salim Groups on October 8, 2021.

title: PGEO’s dividend up 328%
body: The dividend of PT Pertamina Geothermal Energy Tbk (PGEO) for the fiscal year (FY) 2023 soared 328% compared to FY 2022 allocation.

title: Foreign buying and selling volumes reach fantastic figures, net foreign volume drops to -3.94 billion shares
body: On Friday, May 31, share buying and selling transactions by foreign investors reached astounding numbers, with foreign buying volume at 12.72 billion shares and foreign selling volume at 16.67 billion shares. The high amount of foreign sell volume led to a drop in the net foreign volume to -3.94 billion shares. The digital company PT GoTo Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO) was the company with the most shares sold, namely 7.34 billion shares, with a net volume of -2.88 billion shares.

title: Metrodata sets capex budget to IDR 630 billion, 20.3% utilised in Q1 2024
body: PT Metrodata Electronics Tbk (MTDL) prepares IDR 630 billion for its capital expenditure (capex) budget for 2024.

title: Benteng Api Technic to set IPO price to IDR 110 per share
body: PT Benteng Api Technic Tbk (BATR), a heat-resistant and insulation material, has set the initial public offering (IPO) price to IDR 110 per share.

title: May 2024 inflation down to 2.84%
body: Indonesia Statistics (BPS) recorded inflation at 2.84% in May 2024 from May 2023 at 4%. Meanwhile, inflation in May 2024, since December 2023 (year-to-date/ytd), has declined 1.16%.

title: Aiming for IDR 25 trillion of sales again this year, Metrodata seeks 10% profit growth
body: Once hindered by pandemic, PT Metrodata Electronics Tbk (MTDL) now aims for revenue target of IDR 25 trillion again this year, or 13% year-on-year (yoy) increase from the revenue reported in 2023 of IDR 22 trillion.

title: BCA reclaims top market cap spot
body: PT Bank Central Asia Tbk (BBCA) has reclaimed its title as the company with the highest market capitalisation in Indonesia Stock Exchange (IDX) last Friday (31/5), following the sudden drop of stock price of PT Barito Renewables Energy Tbk (BREN).

title: Special this year, MTDL to allocate 39.6% of its 2023 net profit as dividend
body: PT Metrodata Electronics Tbk (MTDL), digital and IT solution provider under Ciputra Group, increases its dividend allocation for FY 2023 to a total of IDR 257.8 billion, increasing approximately 45% from dividend allocation of FY 2022.

title: MEDC’s total dividend went up 7.76% to USD 70.4 million
body: PT Medco Energi Internasional Tbk (MEDC) reported total dividend for the fiscal year (FY) 2023 of USD 70.4 million, 7.76% higher from FY 2022 allocation of USD 65 million.

title: Semen Baturaja to allocate IDR 24.31 billion for cash dividend
body: PT Semen Baturaja Tbk (SMBR), one of state-owned cement manufacturer, is allocating 20% of its 2023 net profit, or IDR 24.3 billion, to be distributed as cash dividend.

title: Chandra Asri Pacific secures loan facility of USD 600 million
body: PT Chandra Asri Pacific Tbk (TPIA), a petrochemical company owned by Prajogo Pangestu, has obtained a senior term loan facility of USD 600 million.

title: Profit down, NICL’s dividend up 175.18%
body: Although the net profit of PT PAM Mineral Tbk (NICL) went down 82.01% year-on-year (yoy) in 2023, the company’s dividend allocation increased 175.18% yoy from the fiscal year (FY) 2022.

title: Consistently improving, RAJA’s dividend soars 139.44%
body: The dividend of PT Rukun Raharja Tbk (RAJA) increased 139.44% in the fiscal year (FY) 2023 from FY 2022, following its net profit surge.

title: Under supervision, BREN’s market cap skyrocketed, up 954.48% since IPO
body: Market capitalisation of PT Barito Renewables Energy Tbk (BREN) saw a dramatic downturn at last Friday’s (31/5) closing bell after recording market cap of IDR 1.5 quadrillion last Monday (27/5).

title: Kimia Farma's loss worsened 6 times in 2023
body: PT Kimia Farma Tbk (KAEF), a state-owned pharmaceutical company through PT Bio Farma (Persero), reported worsening loss in 2023, down to IDR 1.48 trillion.

title: Profit slipped 86%, NAYZ's dividend only set to IDR 306 million
body: PT Hassana Boga Sejahtera Tbk (NAYZ), a processed food manufacturer listed on the stock market on February 6, 2023, only managed to record net profit of IDR 307.55 million in 2023.

title: Starlink enters Indonesia, Mitratel reveals plans
body: PT Dayamitra Telekomunikasi Tbk (MTEL), also known as Mitratel, does not seem bothered with Starlink entering Indonesia’s premises, and is reported to have agreed upon partnership with this satellite connection provider owned by Elon Musk.

title: In need for expansion funding, dividend payout ratio of Mitratel dropped to 75%
body: PT Dayamitra Telekomunikasi Tbk (MTEL), a subsidiary of PT Telkom Indonesia Tbk (TLKM), is planning dividend distribution for the fiscal year (FY) 2023 of IDR 1.5 trillion, or 75% of total net profit of 2023.

title: Net foreign volume at -545 million shares, with foreign transactions exceeding 4 billion shares
body: Foreign investors traded approximately 4 billion shares on Thursday, May 30. Foreign purchases amounted to 4.11 billion, while sales totaled 4.65 billion shares. As a result, net foreign volume closed at -545.06 million shares.

title: Merck readies IDR 143.36 billion for cash dividend
body: PT Merck Tbk (MERK), a pharmaceutical company under Merck Group, announces its plan of dividend distribution of IDR 143.36 billion or IDR 320 per share taken out of its net profit of 2023.

title: After stock split, Ragam Venturinsdo now owns 128 million shares of Asuransi Ramayana
body: The increase in shares recorded by PT Ragam Venturindo due to the stock split of PT Asuransi Ramayana Tbk (ASRM) triggered market hints on Tuesday, May 28. Ragam Venturindo owns roughly 10.57% of ASRM shares, but the loss insurance issuer's 1:4 stock split increased its shareholding from 32.15 million to approximately 128.60 million shares.

title: Performance kept slipping, Yulie Sekuritas wishes for stock market upturn
body: PT Yulie Sekuritas Indonesia Tbk (YULE) still reported slowing down in its performance, both top line and bottom line, in 2023, due to meagre result from all revenue source.

title: Global Mediacom seeks IDR 1.3 trillion through bond and sukuk issuance
body: PT Global Mediacom Tbk (BMTR), an issuer under MNC Group, will issue Shelf-Registration Bond IV of Global Mediacom Phase II Year 2024 and Shelf-Registration Sukuk Ijarah IV Phase II Year 2024 with total principal of IDR 650 billion each.

title: Eyeing USD 40 million per year, BREN to boost geothermal plant capacity
body: Star Energy Geothermal, a subsidiary of PT Barito Renewables Tbk (BREN), will boost the capacity of its geothermal power plant by 116 megawatt (MW) in operational areas of Salak, Darajat, and Wayang Windu.

title: MSTI to share dividend of IDR 313.38 billion
body: PT Mastersystem Infotama Tbk (MSTI) allocates dividend of IDR 313.38 billion for the fiscal year (FY) 2023.

title: Cikarang Listrindo to schedule final dividend distribution of USD 43.12 million
body: PT Cikarang Listrindo Tbk (POWR), an independent electricity provider with the longest operational period in Indonesia, plans a final dividend distribution of USD 43.12 million or IDR 43.91 per share.

title: Optimizing geothermal plants, PGEO and PLN to join hands
body: PT Pertamina Geothermal Energi Tbk (PGEO) has signed a Joint Development Agreement (JDA) alongside PT PLN Indonesia Power (PLN IP) to optimize the capacity of geothermal power plants in Indonesia.

title: Siloam Internasional Hospitals to distribute cash dividend of IDR 260 billion
body: PT Siloam Internasional Hospitals Tbk (SILO), a healthcare provider under Lippo Group, will distribute cash dividend of IDR 260 billion or IDR 20 per share, which will be taken out of its net profit of 2023.

title: Foreign selling volume reaches 4 billion shares, net foreign volume drops to -721.98 million shares
body: Foreign share sales transactions increased on Wednesday, May 29, with a total volume of 4.05 billion shares. Meanwhile, foreign buying volume was only 3.33 billion shares, which caused the net foreign volume to drop to -721.98 million shares. Furthermore, almost 35% of foreign sales volume was generated by the sale of shares in the digital company PT GoTo Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO), totaling 1.43 billion shares, resulting in a net volume of -96.28 million shares.

title: Panin Sekuritas owns 5.17% of Energi Mega Persada (ENRG) after purchasing 1.28 billion shares
body: Bank Panin's subsidiary of a securities company, PT Panin Sekuritas Tbk, recently acquired 5.18% of shares in PT Energi Mega Persada Tbk (ENRG), after purchasing 1.28 billion shares on Monday, May 27. On the other hand, PT Shima Global Kapital, which owns 23.42% of ENRG, sold approximately 450.72 million shares, reducing its stake to 21.6%.

title: Foreign net volume remains stable in green zone and closed at 741.38 million shares
body: Foreign net volume on Wednesday, May 28, remained consistent in the green zone, closing at approximately 741.38 million shares. The foreign buying volume was 4 billion shares, while the foreign selling volume was 3.26 billion shares. Just like the previous day, shares of the digital company PT GoTo Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO) remained at the top, with the greatest share buy of 1.98 billion shares and a net volume of 733.97 million shares.

title: 6 teams from different countries compete in answering nickel mining sector challenges
body: A total of 6 finalist teams of the Global Case Competition held by the Harry Susilo Institute for Ethics in the Global Economy, met in the final round held on Thursday (30/5) today in Jakarta.

title: Establishing 4 new factories, CLEO to increase production by 1 billion litre per year
body: Bottled mineral water manufacturer of Cleo brand, PT Sariguna Primatirta Tbk (CLEO), plans to add 4 new factories this year to increase production capacity as market demand skyrockets.

title: Low energy in domestic automotive scene, Garuda Metalindo eyes 50% growth in 2024 export
body: Dealing with the idle domestic market, PT Garuda Metalindo Tbk (BOLT), a nuts and bolts manufacturer for automotive spare part, is looking for other opportunities in the global market by targeting export sales to increase by up to 50% this year.

title: Maintaining double-digit growth, CLEO to channel dividend of IDR 5 per share
body: PT Sariguna Primatirta Tbk (CLEO), bottled mineral water manufacturer under Tanobel Group, prepares cash dividend distribution of IDR 60 billion for the fiscal year (FY) 2023.

title: Boosting production, Wismilak to allocate capex of IDR 200 billion
body: PT Wismilak Inti Makmur Tbk (WIIM) is allocating capital expenditure (capex) of IDR 200 billion to bump up the production capacity of processed tobacco this year.

title: Free float rate deadline for PermataBank is extended to October
body: Financial Authority Services (OJK) and Indonesia Stock Exchange (IDX) have approved extended deadline for PT Bank Permata Tbk (BNLI) to meet its free float requirement until October 7, 2024.

title: WOM Finance to issue Bond of IDR 1 trillion
body: PT Wahana Ottomitra Multiartha Tbk (WOMF) or WOM Finance is offering its Shelf-Registration V Phase I Year 2024 worth IDR 1 trillion until June 11, 2024.

title: Performance improved in 2023, BOLT’s dividend up 40%
body: Shareholders of PT Garuda Metalindo Tbk (BOLT) agreed upon cash dividend distribution of IDR 35 per share for the fiscal year (FY) 2023 during AGMS held Thursday (30/5).

title: WKR to restructure MTN 2022 in BJBR
body: PT Waskita Karya Realty (WKR), a subsidiary of PT Waskita Karya (Persero) Tbk (WSKT), is restructuring its Medium Term Notes (MTN) III with PT BPD Jawa Barat dan Banten Tbk (BJBR).

title: PTRO secured investment and working capital credit worth over IDR 7.5 trillion
body: PT Petrosea Tbk (PTRO) has secured an investment and working capital credit worth over IDR 7.5 trillion from PT Bank Central Asia Tbk (BBCA).

title: DSSA aims to secure IDR 1.5 trillion through bond and sukuk issuance
body: PT Dian Swastatika Sentosa Tbk (DSSA), an energy company under Sinarmas Group, will issue Shelf-Registration Bond I Phase II Year 2024 and Shelf-Registration Sukuk Mudharabah I Phase II Year 2024 with total principal of IDR 1.5 trillion.

title: UCID's dividend went up 38.54% for FY 2023
body: The dividend of PT Uni Charm Indonesia Tbk (UCID) increased 38.54% in the fiscal year (FY) 2023 compared to FY 2022 dividend.

title: Bata resumes series of losses, reaching IDR 13.86 billion in Q1
body: PT Sepatu Bata Tbk (BATA), the manufacturer of a renowned shoe brand, Bata, booked a loss of IDR 13.85 billion in Q1 2024.

title: Operational costs swelled, Samator Indo Gas saw profit down 8.17%
body: PT Samator Indo Gas Tbk (AGII), a gas supplier for industrial needs, booked net profit of IDR 38.55 billion in the first quarter (Q1) of 2024, down 8.17% year-on-year (yoy) or from the same period last year.

title: Eyeing revenue growth, Autopedia to expand business to used motorcycle
body: PT Autopedia Sukses Lestari Tbk (ASLC) will branch out to used motorcycle sales business line in order to boost future revenue.

title: Skyrocketing net profit, Mandom Indonesia readies IDR 37.8 billion as dividend
body: PT Mandom Indonesia Tbk (TCID), a manufacturer and distributor of skin care products, is scheduling a cash dividend distribution of IDR 37.8 billion or IDR 94 per share taken out of its net profit in 2023.

title: Bank UOB Indonesia seeks fresh funds of IDR 200 billion through bond issuance
body: PT Bank UOB Indonesia, a branch of United Overseas Bank in Indonesia, will initiate the issuance of Shelf-Registration Bond IV Phase I Year 2024 and Subordinated Shelf-Registration Bond IV Phase I Year 2024 with total principal of IDR 200 billion.

title: Profit down, EKAD’s dividend remains stable
body: PT Ekadharma International Tbk (EKAD) will distribute cash dividend of IDR 31.44 billion in the fiscal year 2023, relatively stable compared to the fiscal year 2022 despite its slight decrease in profit.

title: Coal mining companies’ face-off: ADRO reigns supreme in Q1 2024
body: PT Adaro Energy Indonesia Tbk (ADRO) booked the highest net profit in Q1 2024 among four coal mining companies with the highest market capitalisation of over IDR 60 trillion as of May 28, 2024.

title: IDX to lift META’s stock suspension regarding cross transaction
body: PT Bursa Efek Indonesia (BEI) or Indonesia Stock Exchange has lifted temporary suspension of PT Nusantara Infrastructure Tbk (META) in the negotiation market, following a stock cross trade, starting from the afternoon session on Wednesday (29/5).

title: Profit soared, BISI to allocate dividend of IDR 240 billion
body: PT Bisi Internasional Tbk (BISI) is setting aside IDR 240 billion as dividend for the fiscal year 2023, up 17.64% from IDR 204 billion allocated in 2022.

title: Ready for expansion, MKAP retains profit for working capital
body: PT Multikarya Asia Pasifik Raya Tbk (MKAP) decides to allocate the entire net profit of 2023, worth IDR 32 billion, as working capital, according to Annual General Meeting of Shareholders held on Wednesday, May 29, 2024.

title: AP I and II to hold Shareholder Meeting regarding integration plan
body: Next week (4/6), PT Angkasa Pura I and II will hold General Meeting of Shareholders in order to sign a transaction document to proceed with the integration plan following principal approval obtained from the Ministry of State-Owned Enterprises (SOE).

title: Incoming bids in last SUN auction hit IDR 47.11 trillion
body: The total incoming bids recorded in the auction of Government Securities (SUN) yesterday (28/5) was recorded at IDR 47.11 trillion, lower than the bids in May 14 auction of IDR 49.42 trillion.

title: PLN saw 53.21% surge in profit to IDR 22.02 trillion
body: PT Perusahaan Listrik Negara (Persero) reported net profit of IDR 22.02 trillion throughout 2023, up 53.21% year-on-year (yoy) from IDR 14.41 trillion seen in the same period in 2022.

title: Millennium Pharmacon to channel IDR 3.85 billion of capital to subsidiary
body: PT Millennium Pharmacon International Tbk (SDPC) has increased its capital investment in one of its subsidiaries, PT Digital Pharma Andalan Indonesia (DPAI).

title: Bank Mestika Dharma to distribute cash dividend of IDR 138 billion
body: PT Bank Mestika Dharma Tbk (BBMD), will channel out cash dividend of IDR 138 billion or IDR 138 billion or IDR 34.27 per share taken out of its 2023 net profit.

title: Wismilak Inti Makmur will distribute IDR 222.32 billion cash dividends
body: PT Wismilak Inti Makmur Tbk (WIIM), a cigarette manufacturer operating since 1962, will distribute cash dividends of IDR 222.32 billion or IDR 107.1 per share from net profit for the 2023 fiscal year.

title: Foreign trading volumes hit 5 billion shares; net foreign volume closed at 225.83 million
body: On Monday, May 27, foreign investors bought a total of 5.41 billion shares. Moreover, the foreign sales volume was also fairly comparable, at 5.18 billion shares. As a result, net foreign volume ended at 225.83 million shares. The digital company PT GoTo Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO) had the most shares purchased by foreign investors, with 3.01 billion shares purchased for a net volume of 670.58 million.

title: Koperasi Konsumen Karyawan Mandiri purchased 2 billion shares of Widodo Makmur Perkasa (WMPP)
body: On May 22, Koperasi Konsumen Karyawan Mandiri Widodo Makmur became the newest investor in PT Widodo Makmur Perkasa Tbk (WMPP), a consumer goods and agricultural commodities company. This investor purchased over 2.18 billion WMPP shares through PT KB Valbury Sekuritas and PT Samuel Sekuritas Indonesia, gaining control of approximately 7.41% of the shares.

title: MITI team up with Interra to operate Silika concession
body: PT Mitra Investindo Tbk (MITI) teams up with Interra Resources Ltd (IRL) to establish a joint venture to manage the Silica sand business.

title: Taking over 51% of TUJ, GRPM to expand distribution network
body: PT Graha Prima Mentari Tbk (GRPM), a distributor of carbonated drinks, will expand its distribution network by acquiring 51% of PT Tri Usaha Jaya (TUJ).

title: Semarop Agung liquidates 300 million Summarecon Agung (SMRA) shares
body: On Tuesday, May 21, PT Semarop Agung sold approximately 300 million shares of property developer PT Summarecon Agung Tbk (SMRA). Semarop Agung's share ownership decreased from 20.97% to 19.15% as a result of the sale. On the other hand, Liliawati Rahardjo, an SMRA individual investor, decided to increase her stake by 16.21 million shares, giving her approximately 5.36% ownership of SMRA.

title: Stock price slipped 16.7%, JECC’s shareholders agreed upon stock split
body: Shareholders of PT Jembo Cable Company Tbk (JECC) have granted approval regarding the plan of stock split in the Extraordinary General Meeting of Shareholders (EGMS) held last week.

title: Distributing IDR 406.06 billion, BFIN’s dividend dropped 55%
body: PT BFI Finance Indonesia Tbk (BFIN) is setting aside dividend of IDR 406.06 billion or 24.70% of total 2023 net profit of IDR 1.64 trillion.

title: Up 150%, JECC to channel dividend of IDR 37.80 billion
body: The dividend of PT Jembo Cable Company Tbk (JECC) soared 150% higher in 2023 compared to 2022.

title: Unstable prospect, TINS got dropped from 4 indices
body: PT Timah Tbk (TINS) has ben dropped as a constituent from four indices at once following major evaluations.

title: Now custodian, BTPN to promote investments in capital market
body: PT Bank BTPN Tbk (BTPN) has now secured approval from Financial Services Authority (OJK) as a custodian bank that assists institutional and individual investors, both local and international ones.

title: 5 new issuers to be added to Sri Kehati Index
body: Five issuers has been listed as new constituents of Sri Kehati Index, replacing 5 others, following a major evaluation.

title: Medco Energi to divest in Area 47 Libya, transferring it to NOC
body: PT Medco Energi Internasional Tbk (MEDC) has reportedly divested its shares in Area 47 contract located in Libya and transferred them to Libya National Oil Corporation (NOC).

title: Sinergi Multi Lestarindo to launch latest innovation in ICI 2024
body: PT Sinergi Multi Lestarindo Tbk (SMLE), a distributor of raw materials for cosmetic as well as food and beverages, will join Indonesia Cosmetic Ingredients (ICI) 2024 that will be held at JIEXPO Jakarta on May 29-31.

title: Net foreign hits 727.57 million driven by 1.3 billion GoTo share purchase
body: The digital company PT GoTo Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO) ranked first on the top buy list after foreign investors purchased 1.38 billion shares on Wednesday, May 22, for a net volume of 802.09 million. These trades accounted for more than a third of the overall foreign purchase volume, totaling 3.06 billion shares. Meanwhile, foreign sales volume was 2.34 billion shares, bringing the net foreign volume surplus to 727.57 million shares.

title: NCKL and Hong Kong Blue Whale forms 2 JVs
body: PT Trimegah Bangun Persada Tbk (NCKL) and Hong Kong Blue Whale International Limited (HKBWI) establish two joint ventures, called PT Cipta Kemakmuran Mitra (CKM) and PT Bhakti Bumi Sentosa (BBS).

title: Q1 performance revealed, AMMN tops 4 metal and mineral companies
body: Four out of seven companies in metal and mineral industry with market capitalisation of over IDR 20 trillion have released its performance report as of Q1 2024.

title: Panin Financial’s net profit rose 26.28% in Q1
body: PT Panin Financial Tbk (PNLF), a subsidiary of PT Paninvest Tbk (PNIN) engaged in business consultancy and management service, booked net profit of IDR 418 billion in Q1 2024.

title: Two years profiting, MAPB still hesitates on dividend distribution
body: PT MAP Boga Tbk (MAPB) still did not decide on dividend distribution for the fiscal year (FY) 2023, although it has secured net profit following improved revenue.

title: FAPA pockets IDR 50 billion in dividend
body: PT FAP Agri Tbk (FAPA) received an interim dividend payment of IDR 50.35 billion from its subsidiary, PT Riau Agung Karya Abadi (RAKA). The value of dividends paid by this subsidiary is higher than the previous year.

title: Debuting, BATR eyes IDR 71.3 billion in IPO
body: PT Benteng Api Technic Tbk (BATR), a manufacturer and distributor of refractory or heat-resistant materials, will conduct its initial public offering (IPO) to raise funds of up to IDR 71.3 billion.

title: Integrating mobile and FBB business, EXCL takes over LINK’s B2C for IDR 1.87 trillion
body: PT XL Axiata Tbk (EXCL) plans to acquire residential business (B2C) unit and rent 3.3 million home passes of PT Link Net Tbk (LINK) for IDR 1.87 trillion in total.

title: SMKL’s dividend slipped 83.33%
body: The dividend for the fiscal year (FY) 2023 of PT Satyamitra Kemas Lestari Tbk (SMKL) dropped 83.33% compared to FY 2022 dividend.

title: CPIN’s total FY 2023 dividend arrives at IDR 2.13 trillion
body: PT Charoen Pokhpand Indonesia Tbk (CPIN) has set aside IDR 2.13 trillion as cash dividend for the fiscal year (FY) 2023, which are equal to approximately 91.94% of 2023 net profit.

title: Multi Medika Internasional secures facility of IDR 80 billion from Bank Aladin Syariah
body: PT Multi Medika Internasional Tbk (MMIX), a distributor and manufacturer of health devices and equipment, has secured a credit facility from PT Bank Aladin Syariah Tbk (BANK) with ceiling set to IDR 80 billion.

title: Wika Gedung to share cash dividend of IDR 9,38 billion
body: PT Wika Gedung Tbk (WEGE), a building construction subsidiary of WIKA, is scheduling a cash dividend distribution worth IDR 9.38 billion or IDR 0.98 per share.

title: Barito Renewables Energy now listed in FTSE Global Equity Index
body: PT Barito Renewables Energi Tbk (BREN), a renewable energy company under Barito Pacific Group, has entered FTSE Global Equity Index under the category of the highest market capitalisation in June 2024.

title: PRAY’s net profit soared 305%, yet no dividend in sight
body: PT Famon Awal Bros Sedaya Tbk (PRAY), the managing company of Awal Bros Hospital, now Primaya Hospital, decided not to distribute dividend for the fiscal year (FY) 2023 despite its profit skyrocketing 305% from FY 2022.

title: Foreign-sell exceeds 5 billion shares, resulting in net foreign volume of -773.48 million
body: On Tuesday, May 21, foreign share sales transactions reached 5.59 billion shares. Apart from that, share purchases were also significant, although the foreign buy only totaled 4.82 billion shares. As a result, net foreign volume concluded at -773.48 million shares. The most shares sold belonged to PT Bank Mandiri (Persero) Tbk (BMRI) in the banking sector, namely 195.45 million shares, with a net volume of -66.40 million shares.

title: MB Investment purchased 1.77 billion ITSEC Asia shares, giving it a direct 27.49% ownership
body: On Monday, May 20, foreign company MB Investment Management Pte Ltd made a new investment in PT ITSEC Asia Tbk (CYBR) by purchasing approximately 1.77 billion shares. The transaction, facilitated by PT OCBC Sekuritas Indonesia, gave the Singaporean investor immediate control of 27.49%. Another foreign investor, Rejuve Global Investment Pte Ltd, then increased its stake in pharmaceutical company PT Pyridam Farma Tbk (PYFA) by nearly 50 million shares.

title: Hillcon Equity stake in Hillcon (HILL) doubled
body: On Friday, May 17, PT Hillcon Equity Management increased its stake in mining holding company PT Hillcon Tbk (HILL) by two-fold. Previously, Hillcon Equity controlled approximately 33.04% of the shares; however, this investor added 851.38 million shares, increasing its share portion to 61.92%. The investor carried out this transaction using three different securities: PT Trimegah Sekuritas Indonesia Tbk, BUT Deutsche Bank AG, and PT CGS International Sekuritas Indonesia.

title: Foreign investors purchase Gojek Tokopedia shares; net foreign volume recorded surplus 434.01 million
body: On Monday, May 20, foreign investors purchased up to 1.07 billion shares in the digital company PT GoTo Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO) for a net volume of 109.07 million shares. This transaction boosted foreign buying volume to 3.51 billion shares. Meanwhile, foreign sales totaled around 3.08 billion shares, resulting in a net foreign volume surplus of 434.01 million shares.

title: Gov’t to set up yet another SUN auction, aiming IDR 22 trillion next week
body: The Indonesian government is once again holding the auction of Government Securities (lit. Surat Utang Negara/SUN) next Tuesday (28/5) with an indicative target of IDR 22 trillion.

title: Samuel Tumbuh Bersama acquires 1.1 billion shares of Nusantara Sawit (NSSS)
body: After collecting 1.7 billion shares of PT Nusantara Sawit Sejahtera Tbk (NSSS) on the previous trading day, PT Samuel Tumbuh Bersama increased its stake in this palm oil producer by 1.1 billion shares. On Thursday, May 16, the shareholding of this investor's shares increased from 7.19% to 11.81%. On the other hand, PT Mitra Agro Dharma Unggul, the owner of 37% of NSSS, reduced its shareholding by 1.1 billion shares, leaving it with approximately 32.54%.

title: PSSI to set up dividend of IDR 251.06 billion
body: PT IMC Pelita Logistik Tbk (PSSI) sets up dividend of the fiscal year (FY) 2023 of IDR 215.06 billion, which will be distributed on June 20, referring to the recording date of Official Register of Shareholders on June 3.

title: MPMX to join EV business hype
body: PT Mitra Pinasthika Mustika Tbk (MPMX) and its subsidiary, PT Mitra Pinasthika Mulia, will branch out to support electric vehicle (EV) business.

title: Citi Indonesia continues to grow, profit jumps 17% in Q1 2024
body: Citibank N.A. Indonesia, or better known as Citi Indonesia, recorded an increase in net profit of up to 17% year-on-year (yoy) to IDR 665.9 billion at the end of March 2024 from IDR 568.71 billion in Q1 2023.

title: Mandiri Tunas Finance issues IDR 1.16 trillion bonds with the highest coupon at 7.25%
body: PT Mandiri Tunas Finance (MTF), a subsidiary of PT Bank Mandiri (Persero) Tbk (BMRI) which operates in the financing sector, issued Shelf Registration Bonds VI Phase III 2024, with a principal amount of IDR 1.16 trillion.

title: DIGI and EMOF I agreed on CPD relaxation
body: PT Arkadia Digital Media Tbk (DIGI) obtained relaxation of the Convertible Performance Debenture (CPD) agreement worth IDR 11.56 billion with the Emerging Media Opportunity Fund (EMOF) I, LP, United States.

title: Bank Neo Commerce secured IDR 14.35 trillion in first quarter third party funds
body: PT Bank Neo Commerce Tbk (BBYB) collected IDR 14.35 trillion in Third Party Funds (DPK) and net credit distribution of IDR 8.86 trillion throughout the first quarter of 2024. The current account and savings accounts (CASA) ratio is approximately 27.60% of the total DPK.

title: Ayam Goreng Nelongso’s net profit soared 52.53% in Q1 2024
body: PT Bersama Mencapai Puncak Tbk (BAIK), the managing company of Ayam Goreng Nelongso franchise network, scored net profit of IDR 3.92 billion in Q1 2024, up 52.53% year-on-year (yoy) from the same quarter last year.

title: Harita Nickel to prepare IDR 400 billion for buyback
body: PT Trimegah Bangun Persada Tbk (NCKL), a nickel mining company popularly known as Harita Nickel, will execute the shares buyback with a budget of IDR 400 billion.

title: Bank Syariah Indonesia to distribute dividend of IDR 855.56 billion
body: PT Bank Syariah Indonesia Tbk (BRIS), a sharia bank owned by several national banks, announced its cash dividend distribution of IDR 855.56 billion or IDR 18.55 per share.

title: ISAT readies dividend distribution of IDR 2.2 trillion
body: Annual General Meeting of Shareholders (AGMS) of PT Indosat Tbk (ISAT) agreed upon the allocation of dividend of IDR 2.17 trillion for the fiscal year (FY) 2023, slightly climbing from IDR 2.06 trillion allocated in FY 2022.

title: BREN continues to soar, market cap reaches IDR 1.49 quadrillion
body: The stock of PT Barito Energy Resources Tbk (BREN) solidly moves forward by 12.91% until Tuesday (21/5), starting from May 2, 2024.

title: Trisula Textile Industries’ profit soared 62% earlier this year
body: PT Trisula Textile Industries Tbk (BELL), a textile and finished fashion product provider, recorded net profit of IDR 6.6 billion in Q1 2024

title: Tempo to focus on data presentation transformation
body: The transformation of news and data presentation will be the new focus of Tempo for the future, thus driving Tempo to continue developing various video and podcast contents to present news in more attractive and accessible fashion.

title: Waskita’s bondholders’ meeting regarding IDR 1.36 trillion bond fell through
body: The General Meeting of Bondholders (GMB) of PT Waskita Karya (Persero) Tbk (WSKT), which supposedly took place on May 16, 2024, failed to meet the requirements.

title: BAUT to set up dividend of IDR 1.02 trillion
body: PT Mitra Angkasa Sejahtera Tbk (BAUT) sets aside 20% of net profit in 2023 as dividend. This allocation is similar to the allocation made in the fiscal year (FY) 2022.

title: OJK closes down 915 illegal financial institutions until April 2024
body: The Financial Services Authority (OJK), through Task Force of Eradication of Illegal Finance Activities (Satgas Pasti), closed down 915 illegal financial institutions from January 1 until April 30, 2024.

title: MPXL secures new contracts of IDR 148.39 billion
body: PT MPX Logistics International Tbk (MPXL) secured bulk cement sale and purchase contracts with PT Victory Utama Beton (VUB) worth IDR 148.39 billion yesterday (20/5).

title: NICE secures 63.24% surge since early 2024
body: The stock of PT Adhi Kartiko Pratama Tbk (NICE) increased 63.24%, starting from its debut on the stock exchange earlier this year until last Monday (20/5).

title: DYAN eyes 10% revenue growth in 2024
body: Although the net profit shifted 32.4% year-on-year (yoy) down in Q1 2024, PT Dyandra Media International Tbk (DYAN) booked meagre growth in Q1 2024 revenue, up 3.5% yoy to IDR 383.4 billion.

title: RALS’s dividend shrinks 3.67% to IDR 296.56 billion
body: The dividend of PT Ramayan Lestari Sentosa Tbk (RALS) for the fiscal year (FY) 2023 slid down 3.67% from the FY 2022 dividend.

title: Net profit of Bali United owner declines 99.95% in the first quarter
body: PT Bali Bintang Sejahtera Tbk (BOLA), the issuer that manages the Bali United and Putra Samarinda football clubs, only posted a net profit of IDR 16.59 million in the first quarter (Q1) 2024, down 99.95% year-on-year (yoy) or from the same quarter last year.

title: Kurniamitra Duta Sentosa to distribute final dividend of IDR 17.6 billion
body: PT Kurniamitra Duta Sentosa Tbk (KMDS) will share final dividend of IDR 17.6 billion or IDR 22 per share, taken out of its net profit in 2023.

title: Provident Investasi Bersama prepares IDR 80.61 billion for buyback
body: PT Provident Investasi Bersama Tbk (PALM), a holding of investment companies under Provident Capital and Saratoga Group, will hold a buyback with a budget of IDR 80.61 billion.

title: Profit dropped 91%, HALO’s foreign investors decide on mass-divest
body: The decline of net profit of PT Haloni Jane Tbk (HALO) that reached 91% year-on-year (yoy) leads to gradual mass-divestment by foreign investors within the past three months.

title: Incoming bids in today’s state sukuk auction hit IDR 16.5 trillion
body: The total incoming bids recorded within the auction of Government Sharia Securities (lit. Surat Berharga Syariah Negara/SBSN) or state sukuk today (20/5) were recorded at IDR 16.5 trillion.

title: META’s controlling shareholder to extend tender offer to June 19
body: Controlling shareholder of PT Nusantara Infrastructure Tbk (META) under Salim Group, PT Metro Pacific Tollways Indonesia (MPTIS), is extending the voluntary tender offer period, starting from today (20/5) until June 19, setting the offering price to IDR 250 per share.

title: BAIK’s shares dropped 71.58% since its debut
body: The stock price of PT Bersama Mencapai Puncak Tbk (BAIK) plummeted 71.58% since its debut in the stock exchange until last Friday’s (17/5) session.

title: 3 months in, MKAP saw 273.91% surge in price
body: The stock of PT Multikarya Asia Pasifik Raya Tbk (MKAP) has increased 273.91% to IDR 430 per share from IDR 115 per share seen during its debut on the stock exchange on February 12, 2024.

title: BIKE to distribute dividend of IDR 12.93 billion
body: After a year in the stock market, PT Sepeda Bersama Indonesia Tbk (BIKE) has decided to set up dividend of the fiscal year (FY) 2023 of IDR 12.93 billion.

title: Stock price still high, DSSA to stock split with 1:10 ratio
body: PT Dian Swastika Sentosa Tbk (DSSA) is planning a stock split with a ratio of 1:10, from IDR 250 per share to IDR 25 per share.

title: MIDI to allocate 30% of net profit as dividend
body: PT Midi Utama Indonesia Tbk (MIDI), the managing company of Alfamidi convenience store network, sets aside 30% of its 2023 net profit as dividend, stable compared to 2022 net profit.

title: Tigaraksa Satria to channel final dividend of IDR 286.57 million
body: PT Tigaraksa Satria Tbk (TGKA), a consumer good wholesale and distribution company, will issue final dividend of IDR 286.57 billion or IDR 312 per share, which will be taken out of its 2023 net profit.

title: Up 19%, MASA sets up dividend of IDR 339.67 billion
body: PT Multistrada Arah Sarana Tbk (MASA) will share cash dividend for the fiscal year (FY) 2023 of IDR 339.67 billion, up 19.34% from FY 2022 dividend of IDR 284.67 billion.

title: Champ Resto Indonesia’s net profit multiply 4 times in Q1 2024
body: PT Champ Resto Indonesia Tbk (ENAK), a food & beverage (F&B) company debuted on Indonesia Stock Exchange (IDX) in February 2022, booked net profit of IDR 15.46 billion in the first quarter (Q1) of 2024.

title: Apexindo Pratama Duta saw 73.53% surge in net profit Q1 2024
body: PT Apexindo Pratama Duta Tbk (APEX), an oil and gas drilling service company operating since 1984, reported net profit of USD 1.77 million in the first quarter (Q1) of 2024.

title: Sumber Alfaria Trijaya to share cash dividend of IDR 1.19 trillion
body: PT Sumber Alfaria Trijaya Tbk (AMRT) will schedule cash dividend distribution of IDR 1.19 trillion or IDR 28.68 per share taken out of its net profit in 2023.

title: Foreign buy and sell remains stable at 4 billion shares; net foreign recorded -93.37 million shares
body: Foreign buy and sell volumes on Friday, May 17, were both in the range of 4 billion shares, with foreign buying volume at 4.37 billion shares and foreign selling volume at 4.46 billion shares. As a result, net foreign volume ended slightly in the red, at -93.37 million shares. The top stock on the top buy list belongs to the telecommunications company PT Smartfren Telecom Tbk (FREN), which was purchased for 85.36 million shares at a net volume of 52.06 million. On the top-selling list, shares of the e-commerce company PT Bukalapak.com Tbk (BUKA) were at the top, with a sales volume of 238.59 million shares and a net volume of -188.67 million shares.

title: Samuel Tumbuh Bersama acquires 1.71 billion shares of Nusantara Sawit (NSSS)
body: On Wednesday, May 15, a new investor, PT Samuel Tumbuh Bersama, purchased 1.71 shares of palm oil producer PT Nusantara Sawit Sejahtera Tbk (NSSS). This securities company now owns roughly 7.19% of NSSS shares. On the other hand, NSSS's controller, PT Mitra Agro Dharma Unggul, released 1.10 billion shares, causing its share portion to fall from 41.79% to 37.17%.

title: Foreign buy reaches 3.67 billion shares: net foreign volume closes with 174.58 million surplus
body: On Thursday, May 16, foreign investors purchased shares and recorded a foreign buying volume of up to 3.67 billion shares. Meanwhile, foreign sales volume was 3.50 billion shares, with the net foreign volume surplus at approximately 174.58 million shares. The media business PT Media Nusantara Citra Tbk (MNCN) had the most shares purchased, with 218.01 million and a net volume of 50.38 million. Apart from that, 77.55 million shares of the advertising and management consultancy company PT Era Media Sejahtera Tbk (DOOH) were purchased, resulting in the highest net volume of 56.76 million shares.

title: Liliawati Rahardjo acquires 5.09% ownership of Summarecon Agung (SMRA)
body: Individual investor Liliawati Rahardjo acquired 5.09% of property developer PT Summarecon Agung Tbk (SMRA) on Tuesday, May 14. This new investor obtained her stake by purchasing 840.71 million SMRA shares from PT Ciptadana Sekuritas Asia and PT Indo Premier Sekuritas.

title: 762 million Gojek Tokopedia (GOTO) shares bought, net foreign volume recorded surplus of 221.67 million shares
body: Foreign investors purchased 762.11 million shares of the digital company PT GoTo Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO) on Wednesday, May 15, for a net volume of 241.53 million. This transaction boosted foreign buy volume to 2.69 billion shares. Meanwhile, foreign sales totaled 2.47 billion shares, resulting in a net foreign volume surplus of 221.67 million shares.

title: Two major investors in Triputra Agro (TAPG) released billions of shares
body: News from PT Triputra Agro Persada Tbk (TAPG), a palm oil and rubber company, drove the stock market on Monday, May 13. Two TAPG foreign investors sold a large number of shares, beginning with Gochean Holdings Incorporated, which sold 1.09 billion shares, increasing its shareholding to 5.39%. Then, Salween Investment Pte Ltd. sold 1.49 billion shares, reducing its ownership to just 7.37%. Even so, PT Persada Capital Investama, the holder of 23% of TAPG shares, increased its shareholding by 999.86 million shares, bringing its ownership to 28.28%.

title: Lawson Indonesia to gain extra capital of IDR 70 billion
body: PT Lancar Wiguna Sejahtera (LWS), a subsidiary of PT Midi Utama Indonesia Tbk (MIDI) that operates Lawson in Indonesia, receives another extra capital of IDR 70 billion.

title: Mark Dynamics to distribute final dividend of IDR 114 billion
body: PT Mark Dynamics Indonesia Tbk (MARK), a glove cast and sanitation product manufacturer, will distribute final dividend of IDR 114 billion or IDR 30 per share.

title: OPPM to issue bonds worth over IDR 4 trillion
body: PT Oki Pulp & Paper Mills (OPPM) will issue bonds worth more than IDR 4 trillion, consisting of conventional bond, global bond, and green bond.

title: Higher finance expenses, Kobexindo Tractors lost IDR 41.91 billion
